{{Quote|This is it. Thyferra. Be careful, team. We've got to hit Imperial targets only. Avoid civilian casualties at all costs. We can't afford to lose those bacta containers! So be careful.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="rs" />}}
The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] '''attack''' on and occupation of [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]] took place during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. The attack—the culmination of several weeks of planning by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kohl Seerdon]]'s to capture the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] supply—was crippling to the Alliance's pharmaceutical resources. Although Seerdon's military resources were greatly diminished by repeated guerrilla strikes from the elite Alliance starfighter wing [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], he was able to invade the planet relatively easily by manipulating the Alliance to divert their military to the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]].

Rogue group was quickly dispatched to reclaim the bacta supply. A fierce battle took place, in which the Rogues succeeded in driving the Imperial forces off the planet, with some minimal civilian casualties. In a last ditch effort, Seerdon joined the battle in his custom [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] to personally confront [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] of Rogue Squadron. Skywalker bested Seerdon in the ensuing dogfight, effectively ending the Thyferra occupation.

==Prelude==
The Imperial Moff Kohl Seerdon had been in the planning stages for an attack on Thyferra for some time. Intel of the Rebel Alliance quickly picked up on this, and so a string of raids on Imperial territory, spearheaded by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], led by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], was commenced in an effort to reduce the cunning Moff's resources.<ref name="rs" />

A [[Battle above Taloraan|raid]] on the Imperial [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas platforms on the gas giant [[Taloraan/Legends|Taloraan]] drastically reduced Seerdon's firepower, as tibanna was used to power [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. Around the same time, a [[Escape from Fest|raid]] on Fest took place resulted in the destruction of Seerdon's weapons research facility and the theft of three [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PT]] walkers. This operation, in particular, enraged Seerdon, who quickly responded with an attack on a [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrilan]] city, home of [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], the [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]] of the Alliance. In retaliation for the sudden attack on Chandrila, Rogue Squadron struck at the massive [[Capacitor (Sullust)|Capacitor]] situated in a volcano on [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. The operation, engineered by [[Kasan Moor]], an Imperial defector who had extensive knowledge of Imperial ground operations, was a success, and the Capacitor was destroyed.<ref name="rs" />

==The attack==
{{Quote|Rogue Squadron, I'm afraid you'll have to cut your celebration short. Moff Seerdon has begun his attack on Thyferra.|General Rieekan, in the aftermath of the Sullust operation|<ref name="rs" />}}
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)}}
While the Alliance forces were stationed on Sullust, Seerdon gathered whatever men and resources he had left and led his final attack on Thyferra. The Alliance's bacta supply was quickly overrun, and a massive ground force was installed in the area. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] of the Alliance notified Rogue Squadron of Seerdon's actions, and Rogue Squadron was quickly deployed to reclaim the bacta. The ensuing battle between Rebel and Imperial forces was disastrous for the Imperials: not a single walker, [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]], [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] encampment, or [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] bunker survived the battle. The Imperials experienced at least 55 casualties; in stark contrast, Rogue Squadron only lost one X-wing, and even with that the pilot, Kasan Moor, managed to survive the crash.<ref name="rs" />

While defending the bacta tanks, Rogue Squadron proceeded to demolish a nearby Imperial bunker, and proceeded to retrieve [[Homing cluster missile|seeker cluster missiles]] in the remains.<ref name="Nintendo Players Guide">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]''</ref>

After the entire Imperial garrison in the area was wiped out, Moff Seerdon decided to confront Commander Skywalker personally. He arrived on the planet in his custom ''Sentinel''-class landing craft, modified for combat purposes. Challenging Skywalker to a one on one dogfight, Seerdon battled the Rogue commander in the skies over his ruined army. As the firefight went on, Seerdon tried to convince Skywalker that he was too talented to waste his prowess with the Alliance and offered him a spot in the Imperial Navy. As it became evident that Skywalker's piloting prowess outclassed Seerdon's, Seerdon vowed that even if the Rebellion won the day, the Empire will still eventually crush the Rebellion. Shortly after, his shuttle exploded in mid air.<ref name="rs" />
{{Endgame}}

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Woooo-hoo! Great job, Rogue Squadron! The Imperials won't get Thyferra now!|Luke Skywalker, following the battle|<ref name="rs" />}}
With the battle won, the Rebel's bacta supply was effectively secured. With only one X-wing lost during the battle, Rogue Squadron was further cemented as the Rebellion's premier task starfighter squadron.<ref name="rs" />

==Behind the scenes==
This [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] level foreshadows the [[Bacta War]], another conflict between Rogue Squadron and wayward Imperial forces bent on controlling the galaxy's bacta supply, seen in the novel ''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''.
